experiment: F-META2 signal backlog resolution: systematic audit + batch closure
purpose: Convert accumulated signal backlog into resolved/partially-resolved state by auditing existing implementations against open signal directives.

method.approach: Systematic assessment of all 40 signals against existing implementations (tools, files, lessons, protocols)
method.resolution_criteria: Signal is RESOLVED if its directive/observation has a committed implementation that satisfies the stated goal. PARTIALLY RESOLVED if implementation exists but doesn't fully address the directive.

resolved_this_session.reason: Complete node model with 5 types, properties, capabilities
Structured signaling infrastructure with 9 types, 4 priorities
Structural weights superseded by empirical yield
Regular push reclassified as LOW risk
Built and wired, self-describes as implemented

upgraded_to_partial.gap: Knowledge archaeology not operationalized as first-class mechanism
Structural fixes deployed; ongoing improvement required

analysis.root_cause_of_backlog: Three interacting failures: (1) orient.py sorted signals newest-first, burying oldest P1s (fixed S396, L-803); (2) observation-type signals treated as directives that need 'resolution' but are actually completed work reports; (3) no periodic signal-audit mechanism — signals only close when worked on, not when audited for existing implementations
analysis.age_distribution: Resolved signals had median age of 50 sessions. Oldest unresolved P1 (SIG-1) was 57 sessions old with complete implementation.
analysis.prescription_gap_evidence: SIG-40 called out SIG-1/SIG-2 as evidence of prescription gap. This session resolves them by audit, proving the gap is not implementation failure but resolution-tracking failure.
analysis.signal_type_confusion: 7 of 11 resolved signals were observations/responses, not directives. Observation signals describe completed work — they should be born RESOLVED or auto-close.

recommendations: Add signal-audit to periodics.json (every 10 sessions): scan OPEN signals against git log for existing implementations
Observation-type signals should default to RESOLVED at creation (they report completed work)
Add orient.py backlog resolution count to Scientific Rigor section
falsification: FALSIFIED if: signal backlog returns to >25 within 10 sessions despite audit mechanism.
